findings.title: Empty / uninstrumented signup funnel is the #1 blocker to the 100/day north star
findings.founder_problem: There is no measurable acquisition happening. Without instrumented top-of-funnel events and a public value moment, no experiment can be evaluated against the 100/day target.
findings.investor_problem: Investor side is not being acquired at all; no investor signup operations recorded.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: signup_velocity.signups_per_day=0, gap_to_target=100, pct_of_target=0; ai_logs had 0 of [url_submitted, login_completed, lookup_signup_completed, checkout_completed] in 7d; internal_events.sampled=0.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Ship a public, pre-signup 'instant investor match preview' so a visitor gets value (a ranked shortlist + one thesis-fit reason) BEFORE creating an account, then instrument every funnel stage. This is the single lever that converts the 0 baseline into measurable signups.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Public URL-submission -> instant match preview -> account-create CTA. Instrument awareness/value_preview/account_created events in ai_logs.

handoff.title: Public instant investor-match preview (value before signup) + funnel instrumentation

findings.title: Investors are drowning in 'vibe-coded slop' — dealflow volume is now a vanity metric
findings.founder_problem: Quality founders are buried under noise and struggle to stand out / earn investor trust.
findings.investor_problem: AI lets software startups fabricate credibility in an afternoon; investors face a surge of low-signal submissions and fewer seed startups graduating to Series A, so they concentrate capital in fewer, stronger bets and need better filtering.

evidence.rss: Sifted (2026-06): 'Investors bemoan vibe-coded product slop'; Crunchbase News: 'AI Is Rewriting What Investors Should Look For In Early Startup Teams'.
evidence.web: Crunchbase/seed reports 2026: 'dealflow volume has become a vanity metric... software startups can fabricate credibility in a single afternoon'; fewer seed->Series A graduations; capital concentrating.

findings.pythh_opportunity: A data-completeness + quality/traction signal score on every startup profile, powering a curated weekly dealflow digest for investors. Opens the investor side of the marketplace (currently 0 acquisition).
findings.suggested_product_shape: Profile 'data completeness score' + signal filter; investor-facing curated digest of high-signal matches.

handoff.title: Data-completeness/quality score + curated investor dealflow digest

findings.title: Founders burn ~6 weeks on a spreadsheet + a month cold-emailing wrong-fit investors
findings.founder_problem: Most founders spend more time finding investors than talking to them: ~6 weeks building a list, ~1 month emailing people who were never going to say yes, with work split across disconnected tools (inboxes, sheets, notes, calendars).
findings.investor_problem: Mis-targeted cold outreach adds to inbound noise (links to F-2026-0622-02).

evidence.web: seedlegals/thevccorner 2026: 'most founders spend more time looking for investors than talking to them... six weeks building a spreadsheet, another month cold emailing people who were never going to say yes'; 'work split across too many disconnected tools'.
evidence.rss: Sifted (2026-06): fintech founders crowdfunded EUR8m in <1hr — founders routing around painful VC outreach via alternative channels.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Pythh's core wedge: instant match preview + 'why this VC' thesis-fit explanation that replaces the spreadsheet-and-cold-email grind with a ranked, reasoned shortlist in minutes.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Match preview returns ranked investors each with a one-line thesis-fit rationale and suggested approach.

handoff.title: Thesis-fit explainer ('why this VC') on every match

findings.title: Targeting must be partner-level, not firm-level — a data gap competitors leave open
findings.founder_problem: Every partner has distinct tastes and convictions; founders waste meetings pitching firms instead of the one aligned partner, and lack partner-level data to do it.
findings.investor_problem: Individual partners get pitched on themes outside their conviction.

evidence.web: thevccorner 2026: 'partner targeting — not firm targeting'; seedblink 'what investors expect from founders in 2026'.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Resolve and score thesis fit at partner granularity (not firm), a differentiator vs firm-level databases (PitchBook, Harmonic). Feeds the match preview and completeness score.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Partner-level profiles with thesis/stage/check-size completeness; match at partner level.

findings.title: Fresh seed dry powder + bifurcated AI-winner market = deployment-timing matching opportunity
findings.founder_problem: In a bifurcated, AI-winner-take-most market founders must time outreach to funds that just raised / are actively deploying, and broaden capital sources.
findings.investor_problem: Newly-raised funds (e.g. Seedcamp $320M) need quality seed dealflow to deploy.

evidence.rss: TechCrunch/Crunchbase/Sifted (2026-06): 'Seedcamp raises $320M new fund'; Crunchbase 'Robotics startups on fire... record numbers 2026'.
evidence.web: Crunchbase: Q1 2026 record ~$297B venture, 81% to AI; Morgan Stanley: >half of founders pursuing broader range of capital sources.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Investor deployment-timing alerts ('this fund just closed / is actively deploying in your sector') for founders, and broader-capital-source matching. Recurring alerts create a retention loop.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Timing signal on investor profiles ('recently raised / actively deploying') surfaced in matches + alert subscription.
